directive: Prove the agentic swarm is directable by assigning the Claude lane to redesign the chart review section and the agentic control dashboard.
summary: The Claude-lane worker completed a read-only UI brief for ClinicalReviewStation and /ops. It recommended an EHR-style chart workstation, dense Palantir/Linear operator grid, critical status strip, retained answer-blind behavior, and durable proof artifacts.
acceptedRecommendations: Keep /ops as the truthful single operator control surface.
Put critical swarm status before deep dashboard panels: blocked goals, heartbeat restarts, stale agents, queued overrides.
Redesign ClinicalReviewStation as an EHR-style workstation with patient banner, chart tabs, cue rail, active panel, rationale, sources, and tutor dock.
Preserve pre-submit answer blindness and expose rationale/tutor only as locked states until answer submission.
Use flat dark ops colors and off-white chart-paper colors; avoid purple, glass, decorative gradients, and ornamental motion.
guardrails: Read-only Claude design lane did not mutate files directly.
No outbound messages or external account actions were taken.
Correct answer, rationale target, and tutor explanation stay locked before submit.
Dashboard shows missing meters honestly instead of inventing live data.
acceptanceChecks: Browser check /ops for directive strip and operator panels.
Browser check chart review for data-claude-redesign marker and clinical decision workspace copy.
